title: Ship stacked Zellij layout
description: Add a repo-owned Zellij layout that starts Yazelix Next with a stacked tiled pane policy, and make yzn launch the Yazelix Zellij fork with that packaged layout.
acceptance_criteria:
layout.kdl is a repo-owned KDL file.
yzn launches the Yazelix Zellij fork with --layout pointing at the package-owned layout.kdl.
The layout uses Zellij native stacked pane semantics for tiled panes.
The installed package includes share/yazelix-next/layout.kdl.
nix flake show, Zellij layout/config checks, nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, and profile refresh pass.
README LOC scorecard is updated.
notes: Runtime fix applied after dogfood failure: replaced --layout with --new-session-with-layout so yzn launched from inside an existing Zellij session starts a child session in Mars instead of adding a parent-session tab; changed NewPane "Stacked" to NewPane "stacked" to match this fork's accepted action argument. Verified with nix flake show, nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, live profile refresh, and wrapper/config inspection. Did not launch Zellij again from Codex after user reported parent-session tab side effects; awaiting user dogfood.
close_reason: Completed: user dogfooding confirms yzn opens well after switching to --new-session-with-layout and NewPane "stacked"; verification and profile refresh passed.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Reopened: Runtime fix: yzn did not open correctly from inside an existing Zellij session because --layout adds a tab to the parent session, and NewPane "Stacked" is rejected by this fork. Switch to --new-session-with-layout and lowercase NewPane "stacked".

title: Enable Yazi zoxide history updates
description: Shift+Z in Yazi uses Yazi's built-in zoxide preset. Enable its update_db setup so directories visited inside the packaged Yazi sidebar also populate zoxide history.
close_reason: Fixed by enabling Yazi's built-in zoxide preset with update_db in the packaged init.lua. Verified flake show, yzn build, temp profile install, and refreshed installed runtime.

title: Load selected Yazi sidebar plugins
description: Load the selected Yazi plugins for the packaged sidebar: auto-layout, git, and starship. Exclude sidebar-state, sidebar-status, lazygit, and zoxide-editor because they depend on wider Yazelix orchestration or extra tool behavior.
close_reason: Implemented selected Yazi sidebar plugins using imported sources, not vendored plugin Lua: auto-layout.yazi from luccahuguet/auto-layout.yazi, starship.yazi from upstream, and Yazelix-patched git.yazi plus yazelix_starship.toml from a sparse fetch of yazelix-yazi-assets. Excluded sidebar-state, sidebar-status, lazygit, and zoxide-editor. Verified flake show, build, built config inspection, temp profile install, and profile refresh.

title: Restore Zellij bars in stacked layout
description: The custom stacked layout dropped Zellij's default tab-bar and status-bar plugin panes. Restore them while keeping the stacked main pane.
acceptance_criteria:
layout.kdl includes a top tab-bar plugin pane.
layout.kdl includes a bottom status-bar plugin pane.
The main pane remains stacked.
The installed package includes the restored layout.
nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, and profile refresh pass.
README LOC scorecard is updated.
close_reason: Completed: user dogfooding confirms the restored Zellij bars look good; layout restores tab-bar and status-bar plugin panes while keeping the stacked main pane; package build, temp profile install, and live profile refresh passed.

title: Decide yzn key remaps
description:
Decide the small first-class key remap set for yzn before adding more bindings. Current owned Zellij config remaps Alt n to Alt m for new stacked panes and uses Alt Shift h to show/hide the sidebar.

Acceptance criteria:
- List the remaps yzn should own, including current Alt m and Alt Shift h behavior.
- Compare against main Yazelix muscle memory, Zellij defaults, Mars/window-manager conflicts, and Yazi/Helix/Nushell keys.
- Mark each candidate as keep, add, change, or reject, with a short reason.
- Choose one owner for each accepted remap, normally config.kdl unless another layer is clearly better.
- Do not implement remaps in this bead unless the user explicitly chooses the final set.

title: Use Nushell as default pane shell
description: Make the packaged Yazelix Next Zellij config start panes with Nushell as the default shell, while keeping the change small and reversible.
acceptance_criteria:
config.kdl declares default_shell using a Nix-substituted Nushell path.
yzn runtime includes Nushell on PATH.
The packaged share/yazelix-next/config.kdl contains an absolute /nix/store nu path.
nix flake show, nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, and profile refresh pass.
README LOC scorecard is updated.
close_reason: Completed: user dogfooding confirms Nushell default shell is acceptable; flake show, build, temp profile install, live profile refresh, and config/wrapper inspection passed.

title: Design minimal Yazi config management
description:
## Context

Yazelix Next now uses Yazi as the left file-manager sidebar. We want users to be happy with real file-manager behavior: copy/cut/paste files and folders, previews, zoxide/fzf, search, open-with behavior, visual selection, bulk operations, themes/icons, and normal Yazi ergonomics. That makes Yazi the pragmatic file-manager choice for now.

The risk is config management. Main Yazelix handles Yazi config through a full managed materialization system: Rust adapter/writer, generated runtime config, TOML/keymap merging, user init appending, managed plugin/flavor import, legacy path rejection, and a separate yazi-assets renderer. Current measured related surfaces were roughly 2.5k LOC in main Yazelix Yazi materialization/docs/plugins, 1.1k LOC in shared import/path/stub support, and 8k LOC in yazelix-yazi-assets. Do not recreate that shape in Yazelix Next without a very strong reason.

## Direction

Keep the boundary simple:

- Yazi owns file management.
- Yazelix Next owns workspace integration.
- Packaged Yazi config owns only the baseline behavior needed for the sidebar to feel good out of the box.
- User customization should be added only when there is a concrete workflow need, not as a generic config-management project.

Preferred future shape, if/when needed:

1. Keep repo-owned packaged Yazi config tiny and explicit.
2. Avoid TOML deep-merge and generated config directories unless forced by a real use case.
3. Prefer narrow extension points over a broad managed Yazi home.
4. If layering is needed, start with the smallest safe surface, probably Lua `init.lua` after packaged setup, then reassess.
5. Record any irreversible ownership decisions before implementation.

## Rejected Or Deferred Alternatives

- Fork Yazi to remove glue: rejected as a default. It inherits upstream churn and still needs Yazelix integration policy.
- Fork Elio now: deferred. Elio is worth evaluating, but forking before proving it is simpler than a tiny bridge is premature.
- Roll our own terminal file manager: rejected for the user-happiness target because expected file-manager features are broad.
- Zellij-plugin file manager: deferred as a long-term idea; if pursued, keep the core independent from Zellij with a Zellij adapter, but do not replace Yazi now.
- Bite the bullet and port main Yazelix materialization: rejected for Yazelix Next simplicity unless future requirements clearly justify it.

## Acceptance Criteria For A Future Implementation

- State the exact user-visible workflow that requires config management.
- Prove why packaged defaults are not enough.
- Add the smallest config surface that solves that workflow.
- Keep the README LOC scorecard honest.
- Do not introduce generated config directories, TOML merging, plugin import, migration logic, or compatibility shims unless each has a concrete acceptance criterion.
- Keep runtime validation focused on observable Yazi behavior, not generic config plumbing.

title: Adopt Mars Yazelix config
description: Wire yzn to a package-owned Mars config root built from mars.toml, with Yazelix font settings, hidden window decorations, and reef split cursor colors.
acceptance_criteria:
yzn launches Mars with MARS_CONFIG_HOME pointing at a package-owned config root.
mars.toml owns the Mars settings, uses the Yazelix Mars font stack, disables window decorations, and hardcodes reef cursor colors using Mars-supported split cursor fields.
README LOC scorecard is updated.
nix flake show, nix build .#yzn, and a temp profile install pass.
Installed yzn profile is refreshed.
notes: Implemented mars.toml as the yzn-owned Mars config. Verification: `nix flake show --all-systems` passed; `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` passed; TOML parse check confirmed confirm=true, bell audio=true, bell visual=true, font=JetBrains Mono, decorations=Disabled; built wrapper exports MARS_CONFIG_HOME=/nix/store/dqa1ldd7lnnwbdphf9ymhv3sa6nnb63z-yzn-mars-config; temp profile install exposed bin/yzn; `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` refreshed installed yzn to /nix/store/gp9l80zvz3skqzhn75wsylzs8x3r38b0-yzn. Leave open until human dogfooding confirms the Mars window looks right.
close_reason: Completed: yzn uses the yzn-owned Mars config with JetBrains Mono, hidden window decorations, reef cursor colors, confirm-before-quit, both bells enabled, verified by Nix checks and user dogfooding.

title: Make yzn-open logging bounded or disableable
description:
yzn-open currently appends diagnostic events to `${YAZELIX_STATE_DIR}/logs/yzn-open.log` with no rotation or disable switch. This was useful for debugging Yazi-to-Helix opens, but it should not become an unbounded always-on log surface.

Acceptance criteria:
- Keep default diagnostics small and bounded, either with simple rotation/truncation or a clear opt-in debug mode.
- Provide a way to disable or minimize yzn-open logging without changing source code.
- Preserve enough failure evidence for Yazi open debugging when explicitly enabled.
- Keep the implementation simple and avoid introducing a logging framework unless the need becomes real.

title: Layer yzn Nushell user config
description: Allow yzn-scoped Nushell config layering without touching normal Nushell config. Packaged env/config should load first, then optional files under ~/.config/yazelix-next/nu via wrapper-generated runtime Nu source files.
close_reason: Implemented yzn-scoped Nushell config layering with packaged files first and optional user env/config files loaded from ~/.config/yazelix-next/nu via scripts/yzn-nu.sh.

title: Launch the Yazelix Zellij fork under Mars
description:
## Context
Extend the tiny Yazelix Next launcher so `yzn` opens Mars running the Yazelix Zellij fork instead of a plain shell. The fork is the temporary Yazelix KGP/APC passthrough Zellij source consumed by main Yazelix, not upstream nixpkgs Zellij.

## Decisions
- Keep this in the flake/package surface only.
- Consume the Yazelix Zellij fork as a non-flake source input.
- Do not add Home Manager, layouts, config generation, plugins, or pane policy in this slice.
- Preserve the per-language LOC scorecard.

## Acceptance Criteria
See the structured acceptance criteria field on this bead.
acceptance_criteria:
- `flake.nix` has a `yazelixZellij` non-flake input pointing at the Yazelix fork branch.
- `packages.${system}.yazelix_zellij` packages that fork from source using nixpkgs' Zellij packaging as the base where possible.
- `yzn` launches Mars with the packaged Yazelix Zellij fork as its command.
- `nix run` and `nix run .#yzn` still use the same launcher app surface.
- README says `yzn` opens Mars running the Yazelix Zellij fork.
- README documents `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` as the installed-runtime update command.
- The per-language LOC scorecard is updated after the Nix and README changes.
- `nix flake show --all-systems` succeeds.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` succeeds or any build blocker is recorded with the exact failing package/dependency.
- A temp profile install using `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` exposes `<tmp>/bin/yzn`.
- The user's installed `yazelix-next` profile entry is refreshed after runtime changes.
- Human dogfooding confirms `yzn` opens a Mars window running the Yazelix Zellij fork before the bead is closed.
notes: Profile refresh evidence: ran `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next`; `nix profile list` now shows `yazelix-next` at `/nix/store/7imysps2q66y7aimv3xv6963hi6g3xhm-yzn`, and `/home/lucca/.nix-profile/bin/yzn` execs Mars with `/nix/store/3frryp6kl6sk7pqwcc0knksyf65411sa-zellij-0.44.3/bin/zellij`.
close_reason: Completed: yzn launches Mars with the packaged Yazelix Zellij fork; nix flake show, nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, zellij --version, profile refresh, and user dogfooding all passed.

title: Add Yazi sidebar pane
description: Add a minimal left Yazi pane to the packaged Zellij layout using packaged yazi, while keeping Yazi config layering out of scope unless needed.
close_reason: Implemented a minimal packaged Yazi sidebar: layout.kdl has a 20% left command pane using a Nix-substituted yazi path, main pane remains stacked, no Yazi config/user layering added. Verified flake show, build, built layout inspection, Yazi store reference, temp profile install, and profile refresh.

title: Ship owned Zellij config remap
description: Add a package-owned Zellij config and make yzn launch the Yazelix Zellij fork with it, starting by moving Zellij's native new-pane binding from Alt+n to Alt+m. This deliberately does not pull in the pane-orchestrator plugin yet; Yazelix-style workspace-terminal behavior remains a later slice.
acceptance_criteria:
config.kdl is a repo-owned KDL file.
yzn launches the Yazelix Zellij fork with --config pointing at the package-owned config.kdl.
Alt+n is unbound from the native new-pane default.
Alt+m runs the native Zellij NewPane action for now.
The installed package includes share/yazelix-next/config.kdl.
nix flake show, zellij setup --check with the config, nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, and profile refresh pass.
README LOC scorecard is updated.
close_reason: Completed: yzn packages and uses config.kdl; config unbinds Alt+n and binds Alt+m to the native Zellij NewPane action; zellij setup --check, nix flake show, nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, and live profile refresh passed.

title: Launch a minimal host-tool Zellij workspace
close_reason: Obsolete planning: created before the user selected the first scope. The accepted baseline is a flake-installed yzn launcher for Mars with a LOC scorecard, not a Zellij workspace yet.

title: Add slim agent protocol from Yazelix rewrite bead
description:
## Context
Add a slim `AGENTS.md` for Yazelix Next that distills the old Yazelix private-rewrite bead guidance into repo-local rules. The file should guide agents without importing the full main Yazelix policy surface.

## Decisions
- Use `yazelix-9hpmy` as the source planning bead and `yazelix-z9kew` as the merged contract-distillation method.
- Keep the guidance short and specific to this repo.
- Emphasize user-led scope decisions, contract distillation, small vertical slices, LOC discipline, Beads, and exact verification.

## Acceptance Criteria
See the structured acceptance criteria field on this bead.
acceptance_criteria:
- `AGENTS.md` exists and is concise.
- `AGENTS.md` captures contract-distillation rules from old Yazelix beads without copying the full main `AGENTS.md`.
- `AGENTS.md` tells agents to use `br` for Beads and not to invent scope before the user decides.
- `AGENTS.md` documents the current runtime chain and installed `yzn` refresh rule.
- README LOC scorecard includes `AGENTS.md` as Markdown.
- `br lint --json` stays clean.
close_reason: Completed: added slim AGENTS.md distilled from yazelix-9hpmy/yazelix-z9kew guidance and updated README per-language LOC scorecard.

title: Add yzn desktop entry
description: Ship a desktop launcher in the yzn package so profile installs expose Yazelix Next in graphical app launchers without Home Manager.
acceptance_criteria:
packages.${system}.yzn contains bin/yzn and share/applications/yzn.desktop.
The desktop entry launches yzn without a terminal, uses a Yazelix Next name, and installs an icon under the yzn icon name.
nix flake show, nix build .#yzn, and temp profile install pass.
README LOC scorecard is updated.
Installed yzn profile is refreshed.
close_reason: Completed: yzn package now ships bin/yzn, share/applications/yzn.desktop, and yzn icons; flake show/build/temp profile install/profile refresh all passed.

title: Simplify yzn runtime plumbing
description: Reduce real LOC and concepts while preserving current yzn behavior: remove fallback/runtime duplication and one-call wrappers where verification proves equivalence.
close_reason: Completed real simplification pass: removed Zellij base compatibility fallback, removed duplicate Nu tool runtime inputs from outer yzn wrapper, and stopped copying generated carapace/zoxide init files into final share output. Rejected prompt inlining because it hurt readability for formatting-style LOC only. Verified flake show, build, direct yzn-nu smoke, temp profile install, and profile refresh.

title: Fix Yazi auto-layout setup
description: The Yazi sidebar loads auto-layout.yazi but does not call setup(), so the layout override is never installed and Yazi still shows multiple columns in the narrow sidebar.
close_reason: Fixed yazi/init.lua to call require("auto-layout"):setup(), matching main Yazelix core-plugin setup behavior so the Tab.layout override is installed. Verified build, built init inspection, temp profile install, and profile refresh.

title: Reuse Helix editor pane from Yazi
description: Make the packaged Yazi opener keep Yazi as the sidebar when creating the first editor pane, then reuse a live Yazelix Helix bridge for later file opens. Keep this as a narrow opener script plus bridge-enabled yzn-hx; do not port main Yazelix pane orchestrator or full control plane.
notes: Final committed Rust helper shape: yzn-open keeps the bridge opener narrow with anyhow, serde, and serde_json only. Follow-up simplification removed fs-err and path-absolutize before commit; standard library filesystem/path APIs are enough for this Linux-only slice.
close_reason: Implemented with a Rust yzn-open helper. Yazi calls yzn-open; yzn-open reuses a live Yazelix Helix bridge via typed serde JSON over a Unix socket and falls back to opening yzn-hx in a right Zellij pane. Used anyhow, fs-err, path-absolutize, serde, and serde_json; skipped interprocess/duct/clap because they did not reduce this Linux-only slice enough. Verified cargo test, nix build, flake show, temp profile install, built Yazi debug, fallback smoke test, and refreshed installed yzn.

title: Verify yzn session and window isolation
description:
Check that separate yzn launches/windows are isolated from each other and do not accidentally share opener, Helix bridge, Zellij session, or Yazi state.

Context:
- yzn generates YAZELIX_HELIX_BRIDGE_SESSION_ID at top-level launch.
- yzn-hx defaults the bridge session id to yzn when the env is absent.
- yzn-yazi also defaults the bridge session id to yzn when the env is absent.
- yzn-open scans ${YAZELIX_STATE_DIR}/helix_bridge/<session_id>/ and uses registry zellij_session_name matching when present.
- Multiple Mars/Zellij windows or externally invoked helpers could expose isolation bugs if env propagation or fallback defaults are wrong.

Acceptance criteria:
- Document the expected isolation contract for one yzn window, multiple yzn windows, and helper commands invoked outside yzn.
- Verify whether yzn, yzn-yazi, yzn-hx, and yzn-open always agree on the intended session id inside a normal yzn window.
- Verify that opening files from Yazi in one yzn window cannot target a Helix bridge in another yzn window.
- Decide whether the yzn-yazi/yzn-hx fallback session id of yzn is acceptable or should fail/derive a safer id when launched outside yzn.
- Add the smallest focused tests or runtime inspections needed to prove the contract.
- Keep the README LOC scorecard updated if implementation changes files.

title: Finalize the flake-installed yzn Mars launcher baseline
description:
## Context
Create the first Yazelix Next baseline as a Nix-compatible flake. The project should expose a short `yzn` command that opens Mars, support both `nix run` and profile installation from a local or absolute path, and keep a visible LOC scorecard so future work has a simplicity budget.

## Decisions
- Use Nix/Lix-compatible flakes as the project interface.
- Use `yzn` to avoid conflicting with the existing `yzx` command.
- Do not add Home Manager yet.
- Do not add Zellij or a workspace layer until the user explicitly chooses that next feature.
- Keep Mars packaging complexity isolated upstream.

## Acceptance Criteria
See the structured acceptance criteria field on this bead.
acceptance_criteria:
- `flake.nix` exposes `packages.${system}.yzn` and `packages.${system}.default` for supported Linux systems.
- `flake.nix` exposes `apps.${system}.yzn` and `apps.${system}.default` so `nix run` opens the launcher.
- The installed package provides `bin/yzn`, and `yzn` execs Mars without claiming the existing `yzx` command.
- README documents `nix run`, `nix run .#yzn`, absolute-path profile install with `--refresh`, and a per-language LOC scorecard.
- The LOC scorecard separates handwritten Markdown, handwritten Nix, and generated JSON lockfile lines.
- `nix flake show --all-systems` succeeds.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` succeeds.
- A temp profile install using `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` exposes `<tmp>/bin/yzn`.
- Human dogfooding confirms `yzn` opens a Mars window before the bead is closed.
close_reason: Completed by the staged baseline and superseding Zellij slice: flake exposes yzn/default apps and packages, README documents run/install/update and per-language LOC, nix flake show/build/temp profile install passed, and user confirmed yzn works.

title: Add Starship, Carapace, and zoxide to Nushell
description: Package Starship, Carapace, and zoxide into the Yazelix Next Nushell runtime, and configure the packaged default shell to use Starship prompt plus generated Carapace and zoxide Nushell init scripts.
acceptance_criteria:
A repo-owned Nu config wires Starship as the prompt.
The packaged Nu config sources generated Carapace and zoxide Nushell init scripts.
yzn default_shell points at a packaged Nushell wrapper that loads the repo-owned Nu config.
yzn runtime includes nushell, starship, carapace, and zoxide on PATH.
The installed package includes the Nu config and generated init scripts under share/yazelix-next.
nix flake show, Nu config/init smoke checks, nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, and profile refresh pass.
README LOC scorecard is updated.
close_reason: Completed: user dogfooding confirms the shell integration is working; Starship, generated Carapace init, generated zoxide init, banner suppression, package build, temp profile install, and live profile refresh passed.

title: Add Yazelix Helix opener
description: Package the Yazelix Helix fork in yazelix-next and configure the packaged Yazi sidebar to open files with it. Keep the slice narrow: no broad Helix config management, no Yazi config layering, and no pane-control bridge unless dogfooding proves it is needed.
notes: Final shape: yzn and yzn-yazi export EDITOR/VISUAL to the packaged yzn-hx wrapper, yzn-hx launches the Yazelix Helix fork with the repo-owned config dir, and packaged Yazi opens text files through Zellij native action edit so Zellij owns pane creation. Avoided direct Yazi-pane Helix launches and avoided a custom pane-control bridge.
close_reason: Implemented: yzn packages the Yazelix Helix fork; yzn-hx launches it with a repo-owned config dir; yzn and yzn-yazi export EDITOR/VISUAL to yzn-hx; packaged Yazi opens text files through Zellij native action edit so Zellij owns pane creation. Verified with nix build, flake show, Helix health, Yazi debug, temp profile install, and refreshed installed yzn.
